name: Fresh Double Berry Pie
recipeIngredient: 5 cups assorted fresh or frozen berries (raspberries and blackberries)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine 1-1/4 cups sugar, cornstarch, and tapioca; stir well.
2. Add the berries and liqueur; toss gently. Set aside.
3. Prepare pie crust according to package directions for a filled one-crust pie using a 9-inch pie plate. Flute edges and bake at 450°F (232°C) for 9 to 11 minutes. If the crust puffs up, gently press it back into the bottom and sides of the dish with the back of a wooden spoon.
4. Unfold the remaining pie crust sheet; sprinkle lightly with flour.
5. Using a 3-inch heart-shaped cookie cutter, cut out enough hearts to overlap around the edge of the pie. Reserve extra pie crust for later.
6. Spoon filling into the prepared pastry shell. Arrange the hearts, overlapping slightly, with tips pointing toward the center of the pie. Cut remaining pie crust sheet into heart shapes using a heart-shaped canape cutter. Place small hearts over the filling (turn them to face the opposite direction from the larger hearts).
7. Place the pie on a baking sheet and bake at 375°F (190°C) for 1 hour.
8. Combine the remaining 1 tablespoon sugar and cinnamon; sprinkle over the pies. Cool completely.
Yield: one 9-inch pie
